description: A designed commemorative propaganda poster from the U.S. Department of Labor marking its founding anniversary. Background is a deep blue sky with clouds over a color photograph of the Frances Perkins DOL headquarters building in Washington, D.C. Large gold numerals dominate the upper center: 'Since' (in a white script font) above '1913' (huge gold serif), with the white all-caps subhead 'SERVING THE AMERICAN WORKER' beneath, and the small DOL seal centered below the text. The building facade is decorated for a patriotic occasion: a giant U.S. flag hangs vertically at center, red-white-and-blue bunting lines the lower floors, and rows of small American flags line the plaza. Crucially, two enormous building-banner portraits are draped on the facade: at left a stylized portrait of President Donald Trump with an 'AMERICA 250' mark and the slogan 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST'; at right a matching banner with a portrait (Frances Perkins / a historical figure) and the same 'AMERICA 250 / AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST' slogan. The carved building name 'FRANCES PERKINS, DEPARTMENT OF LABOR BUILDING' is visible. The visual style is grand, patriotic, and reverential (monumental architecture, gold typography, flags). The messaging technique fuses the agency's 1913 institutional heritage with current Trump-administration branding ('AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST', 'AMERICA 250' semiquincentennial), placing Trump's portrait literally on the historic building. Target audience: the general public and the administration's worker-base constituency. Tweet text: 'Today, we renew our commitment to helping @POTUS put AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST.'
notable_text: Since 1913 — SERVING THE AMERICAN WORKER; AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST; AMERICA 250
